steps.title: Substitute and apply the given condition
steps.content:
We have $g(x) = b - 12x$. Substituting $x = c+5$:
$$g(c+5) = b - 12(c+5) = b - 12c - 60$$
We're told that $g(c+5) = \frac{c}{3}$, so:
$$b - 12c - 60 = \frac{c}{3}$$

steps.title: Solve for b and simplify
steps.content:
Add $12c + 60$ to both sides:
$$b = \frac{c}{3} + 12c + 60$$
Convert to a common denominator: $12c = \frac{36c}{3}$:
$$b = \frac{c}{3} + \frac{36c}{3} + 60 = \frac{37c}{3} + 60$$
The correct answer is choice C.
